response.justification: While the candidate has extensive software development experience across multiple technologies, they fundamentally lack the mandatory requirements for this role. The C++ experience is limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments plus thesis work—far short of the required 5–10 years. Windows platform development is not mentioned anywhere. Most critically, there is zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domain expertise. The career trajectory shows strong preference for web technologies and emerging languages (D-Lang), not the enterprise financial systems specialization required.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software development across diverse domains and technologies demonstrates strong adaptability
Proven ability to master new languages and frameworks, plus expertise in Java (a secondary asset for financial systems)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by multiple workshops, talks, and technical mentoring roles
concerns: C++ experience totals approximately 6 months at National Instruments plus thesis work—far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
No mention of Windows platform development experience, which is a mandatory requirement for the role
Zero demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domains—lacks both required paths to domain expertise
